description: Substantial distance inward or downward from a reference surface is 'nulo'. The established expression 'nulo sano' means deep knowing, so the same spatial adjective can characterize understanding. 'Theru' (thick) measures between opposing surfaces, while 'nusho' (depth) names a distance that can be measured.
articulatory_notes: The tongue tip contacts the upper teeth for dental nasal 'n', with voice resonating through the nose into rounded high-back 'u'. The tongue tip then meets the alveolar ridge for 'l' before rounded mid-back 'o'. Stress falls on 'nu'.
sound_symbolism: Both vowels in 'nulo' are formed toward the back of the mouth with rounded lips. That compact, inward contour offers a mnemonic for distance below a surface.
usage_notes: The phrases 'nulo phialu' and 'nulo sano' mean deep water and deep knowing. In a noun position, 'nulo' names the quality of being deep; a measured depth is 'nusho'.

semantic_domains.spatial: substantial distance inward or downward from a reference surface
semantic_domains.wisdom: sustained inquiry or knowing that reaches beneath an initial account
